events.description: Board of Indian Commissioners forwarded batch of replies regarding condition of patent-in-fee Indians to Commissioner
Superintendent J.C. Hart submitted report on releasing Indians from Government supervision covering Pawnee, Ponca, Otoe, Kaw, and Tonkawa tribes
Widespread sale of allotted land by Indians who received fee patents
Spending of land sale proceeds on automobiles, high living, gambling, and whiskey
Death of several allottees including Rosa Burnett, Anna Hardy, G.M.C. Hardy, David Frankier after losing land and funds
Lindsay Thompson jailed for drunkenness; Hugh Thompson jailed for running still; Dadie Pappan arrested (possibly innocent)
Various allottees married white spouses or remarried after husbands left
Moses Bellmard served as 1st Lieutenant in WWI
Various allottees working in oil fields, railroads, teamster work, farming
Health survey at Ponca City showed high syphilis rates; Benjamin Frankier paralyzed on one side; Laura P. Randall old and feeble
Warren Harris willed allotment to four minor sisters before death

financial_transactions.description: Sale of land by Maggie LaDue (née Mitchell)
Mortgage on land by Ben Told, also leased for five years
Systematic sale of land by patent-in-fee Indians, proceeds spent on automobiles and non-essentials
Leasing of land for oil, gas, and agricultural purposes
Purchase of high-powered automobiles, expensive Indian toggery, gambling, whiskey
Funds divided among relatives leading to dependency
Monthly payments to old dependent Indians who had lost their land

fee_patents.acreage: 340 acres surplus sold, 160 homestead deeded to wife

correspondence.subject: Forwarding batch of replies regarding condition of patent-in-fee Indians
Releasing Indians from Government Supervision - Reports from five agency farmers
correspondence.action_requested: Information on effects of fee patents
correspondence.outcome: Submitted reports showing generally negative outcomes

correspondence.subject: Condition of Kaw Indians who received fee patents
correspondence.outcome: Reported approximately three-fourths of young adult Indians received patents with harmful results

correspondence.subject: Effects of fee patents on Tonkawa Indians
correspondence.outcome: Reported only one Indian (Lona Ciserneos) accumulating property, most others wasted funds

legislative_actions.outcome: Policy allowing issuance of fee patents (certificates of competency) to Indians, removing restrictions on land
Kaw Treaty provisions requiring written applications for competency certificates
North Carolina cases establishing doctrine of 'state of pupillage' for Indians
